actionUnits.condition: Player has Rigidbody (Has_Rigidbody: true) and a BoxCollider (id: 1121179988, enabled). MonoBehaviour id 1121179983 on Player is the exact serialized settings of the attached SpacialMovement script (m_Script fileID: 11500000, guid: 31b5de8685012e642b97f59bb31caf8d). Serialized fields: moveSpeed=1, rotationSpeed=2, limitX=[490,560], limitY=[3.5,15], limitZ=[515,570], delayToStartMovement=2. This Trigger waits slightly longer than delayToStartMovement so that Start() invokes startMovement() via Invoke, flipping allowMovement to true and exercising Update() logic without user input (movement, rotation, clamping, and escape check paths). No UnityEvent m_Calls are defined on this component, so no direct method calls are queued.

actionUnits.condition: MoonController (MonoBehaviour id: 227156925) on GameObject 'Moon' is the exact serialized configuration of the provided script (m_Script fileID: 11500000, guid: 243873248f8e3f6468b009a9e2d86557). In the scene meta, its 'player' field references the Player's Transform (fileID: 1121179986). Moon has no Rigidbody/Collider/EventTrigger, so we trigger time progression via Player (which has Rigidbody/Collider) to execute MoonController.Update(), which orients Moon to look at Player each frame. No UnityEvent m_Calls are defined on MoonController, so there are no direct method calls to invoke.
